question_title: Will the SPC issue a Day 1 Moderate Risk (or higher) convective outlook on any day from July 1–9, 2026?
summary: Modern-era base rate for at least one SPC Day 1 Moderate Risk (or higher) day in the July 1-9 window is ~18% (2 of 11 years, 2015-2025), the appropriate reference since the 2014 Enhanced-category reform raised the MDT bar. 2026 features a genuinely active, derecho-favorable pattern: a heat-dome 'ring of fire,' July being peak derecho month, and a forecast ridge breakdown after July 4 boosting organized severe potential in the July 5-9 window. This lifts the probability above the unconditional base rate. However, MDT remains a high bar that most active summer patterns fail to reach (they top at Enhanced), and current SPC outlooks are only Slight/Marginal with no near-term MDT signal. Net estimate ~30%.

factors.description: Modern-era base rate (2015-2025): 2 of 11 years had a MDT+ day in July 1-9, the correct reference under current standards.

Active classic derecho-favorable 'ring of fire' heat-dome pattern; July is peak derecho month (37%); ridge breakdown after July 4 expected to boost organized severe potential in July 5-9. Clearly more active than an average year.
MDT is a high bar even in active patterns; most ring-of-fire events top at Enhanced (~82/yr) not Moderate (~14/yr); current SPC outlooks only Slight/Marginal.
Full-record base rate (30%) and small modern sample (2 hits) suggest 18% may slightly understate the true rate.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 30% (NO favored)**

The question asks whether SPC will issue a Day 1 Moderate Risk (or higher) convective outlook on any day July 1–9, 2026.

**Reasoning:**
- **Base rate (anchor): ~18%** — Under modern standards (post-2014 Enhanced-category reform), only 2 of the last 11 years (2016, 2022) had a MDT+ day in this exact 9-day window. The full record shows 30%, but that's inflated by pre-reform years when the MDT bar was lower.
- **Upward pressure:** 2026 has a genuinely active, derecho-favorable pattern — a heat-dome "ring of fire," July being the peak derecho month, and a forecast ridge breakdown after July 4 that should boost organized severe potential in the July 5–9 window. This is clearly an above-average year.
- **Downward pressure:** MDT is a high bar; most active summer ring-of-fire patterns top out at Enhanced (~82 days/yr) rather than Moderate (~14/yr), and current SPC outlooks are only Slight/Marginal with no near-term MDT signal.

Net: the active pattern lifts the estimate meaningfully above the ~18% base rate to **30%**, while MDT's structural rarity keeps it well below a coin flip.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if any official SPC Day 1 Convective Outlook issued and valid for any of the nine calendar days July 1, 2026 through July 9, 2026 (using the outlook's valid date) assigns a categorical risk of Moderate (MDT, level 4/5) or High (HIGH, level 5/5) to any area within the contiguous United States. Otherwise it resolves NO. The categorical outlook and its highest risk level can be checked for each day via the SPC Day 1 outlook page (https://www.spc.noaa.gov/products/outlook/) and the dated archive (https://www.spc.noaa.gov/archive/). A Moderate or High categorical area appearing in any one of the multiple daily issuances for any of these days is sufficient.
fine_print: "Day 1 Convective Outlook" refers specifically to the categorical convective (severe thunderstorm) outlook product, not the Day 2, Day 3, Day 4–8, fire weather, or thunderstorm outlook products. A day counts if any of that day's Day 1 issuances (including updates) contains a Moderate or High categorical risk area. The valid date is determined by the calendar date (UTC) of the outlook's valid period as labeled by SPC. If the primary outlook page and archive are both unavailable, resolution will rely on equivalent records reported by NOAA/NWS or other credible sources (https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions).

factors.description: Modern-era base rate (2015-2025, post-Enhanced-category reform): 2 of 11 years had a MDT+ day in July 1-9 window. This is the correct reference class for current resolution standards.

Active classic derecho-favorable 'ring of fire' heat-dome pattern in place; July is peak derecho month (37% of warm-season derechos); ridge breakdown after July 4 expected to boost organized severe potential in July 5-9 window. This year is clearly more active than the average year.

MDT remains a high bar even in active patterns; most ring-of-fire events top out at Enhanced (~82 days/yr) not Moderate (~14/yr); current SPC outlooks only Slight/Marginal with no near-term Enhanced posted.
Full-record base rate (30%) and small modern sample (only 2 hits) suggest 18% may slightly understate true rate.

input.anchor: Modern base rate 2015-2025: 2 of 11 years (~18%) had a Day 1 MDT+ in July 1-9 (IEM/SPC archive)
input.assessment: Anchor is the modern base rate of ~18% for at least one MDT+ day in July 1-9. The 9-day window plus a genuinely active derecho-favorable pattern (heat dome, ring of fire, ridge breakdown July 5-9) pushes this above the unconditional base rate. However, MDT is a high bar that most active summer patterns fail to reach (they top at Enhanced). Net: ~30%. The dramatic derecho narrative is well-publicized, so I apply caution against overweighting it — but the pattern signal is real and confirmed by multiple sources. The window being a full 9 days, covering both the pre-July-4 ring-of-fire and the post-July-4 ridge breakdown, is a meaningful boost over a single-year average.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether the active pattern produces a well-organized enough event to warrant MDT (vs topping at Enhanced). The modern sample is small (only 2 hits in 11 years).
input.tool_audit: research() returned excellent structured data with verified SPC archive citations and a clean modern-vs-full-record base rate distinction. Both queries succeeded. No failures.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was ideal here — it produced a clean base rate with the critical Enhanced-category-reform caveat that I would have missed, plus a current-pattern read. Minimal friction; the climatological decomposition was exactly what a threshold-style frequency question needs.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
I verified all four factors against the research trace and worldview store. No errors found.

**Factor grounding (all supported by trace):**
- Factor 1 (modern base rate 2/11 years, ~18%, 2015-2025): Trace states verbatim "Under the modern definition, the relevant base rate is 2 of 11 years (~18%) for 2015–2025 (only 2016 and 2022)." Cross-validated against primary SPC archive. ✓
- Factor 2 (ring of fire, July 37% derecho peak, ridge breakdown July 5-9): Supported — trace cites heat dome ring of fire, "July is peak month for U.S. derechos (~36.7%)", "ridge forecast to break down after July 4," AccuWeather 95mph derecho warning, and confirms active regime via June 20 Enhanced Risk. ✓
- Factor 3 (MDT high bar, ~14/yr vs Enhanced ~82/yr, current Slight/Marginal): Supported by trace data_points (14 MDT days/yr, 82 Enhanced days/yr) and current outlook status. ✓
- Factor 4 (full-record 30%, small modern sample): Supported — trace gives 30.4% (7/23). ✓

**Resolution alignment:** Criteria are provided and clear (Day 1 categorical, MDT level 4/5 or HIGH, July 1-9 valid date). Factors engage directly with these specific terms. No misalignment. Not a pre-publication-event case — the window starts July 1, after the June 29 publication, and the agent correctly forecasts forward-looking probability rather than treating any past event as resolving.

**Logit/probability check:** Factor sum = -1.5 + 0.8 - 0.3 + 0.2 = -0.8 logit ≈ 31%, consistent with the 30% estimate. The ~12pp departure from the 18% anchor is driven by the active-pattern factor (+0.8), which is well-sourced (confirmed heat dome, peak derecho climatology, recent Enhanced confirming active regime) — not narrative-only. My independent estimate lands in the 25-32% range: the active pattern over a 9-day window in peak derecho season genuinely lifts above the unconditional base rate, while MDT's rarity (most ring-of-fire events top at Enhanced) caps the upside. 30% is well-justified.

**Counterargument:** Strong and genuine — constructs the real NO case (9/11 modern years had zero MDT, MDT rarity, current Slight ceiling, SPC not yet signaling confidence) rather than restating the conclusion negatively.

**Self-confidence:** 0.75 is calibrated given irreducible 2-10 day meteorological lead-time uncertainty.

**Tool audit:** The claim "Both queries succeeded. No failures" refers specifically to the research() tool, which ground truth confirms (1 call, 0% errors; result shows successful_count: 2, failed_count: 0). The session-wide errors (fetch_url 6.2%, web_search 12.5%) belong to sub-tools not referenced by this narrow audit statement, so the claim is accurate as scoped.

No double-counting, no wrong-direction factors, no hallucinations, no worldview contradictions (the two backing research entries are fresh and consistent). Approve.
